commit.message:
Integrate assurance frameworks and pragmatic corpus workflows

Align the three papers around typed authorization, evidentiary verdicts, and projectibility-first evaluation; add commitment-protected Study B fixtures and validators; add privacy-minimized naturalistic-corpus tooling, synthetic fixtures, source custody records, and editorial cleanup.

Co-Authored-By: Codex <noreply@openai.com>

Integrate context instability into assurance programme

Add Zhang's aggregation result without changing Study A, incorporate Zhang and Rajagopalan into the delegation and evidentiary assurance papers, and specify a projectibility-first Study B design with controlled reconstructions.

Co-Authored-By: Codex <noreply@openai.com>

Ship hiring-facing Study A checkpoint

Add a concise evaluation brief and fresh-clone public checks, align the Study A materials with the pending HREB determination and object-only analysis contract, and harden the fail-closed collection gate with regression coverage. Regenerate semantic stamp 2 while keeping external returns closed.

Co-Authored-By: Codex <noreply@openai.com>

Harden Study A freeze and collection contract

Repair role-separated packaging, assignment provenance, support reporting, and production comparison inputs. Add semantic stamp-2, judge-condition, tagged-scope, and operational collection gates with adversarial regression coverage, and align the paper and pilot terminology with panel-relative claims.

Co-Authored-By: Codex <noreply@openai.com>

Repair Study A freeze analysis contract

Remove the invalid gold-direction estimand, reframe paired outcomes, align panel-relative outputs and prose, and strengthen synthetic regression coverage. Rebuild the ten-artifact pre-freeze manifest.

Co-Authored-By: Codex <noreply@openai.com>

Resolve Study A freeze re-verify board findings (gold-direction, robustness split, limitations)

Focused board (Chen, hostile methodologist, Wallace) re-checked the estimand
revision; all three confirmed their prior defect fixed with no new correctness
bugs, plus one narrow follow-up (Wallace). Adds a pre-registered gold-direction
match estimand (S5) alongside reference-flip (C5/C6), splits the author-label
comparison by reference robustness (unanimous/majority) per plan C3/C4, and
records disclosed non-blocking limitations (prompt-robustness, the
compliant<->no_policy_or_authority_limit label boundary, taxonomy crosswalk,
no system tier, zero-variance policy gold). Manifest regenerated.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Execute Study A estimand revision (5A+5B+6A+7A)

Role-separates the judge into linguistic and policy passes (7A), splits
references by type with a contested-items export (6A), adds per-pair
authority-sensitivity reporting (5A), and reports policy_compliance
agreement on the policy-at-stake subset (5B). Regenerates both judge
comparators and the freeze manifest; analysis-plan.md updated to match.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix freeze-manifest git_commit self-reference; add freeze-readiness review board

Six-reviewer Opus board returned unanimous NO-GO on tagging the Study A
pre-registration. Mechanical fixes applied now: drop the manifest's
git_commit field (a manifest can't reference the commit that contains
it, and recording the parent commit made --verify fail at that commit),
and add benchmark/items/seed-items.csv to the frozen artifact set (it
grounds the Option-B leak argument and was missing). Estimand-level
blockers (co-primary measures, disagreement typology, judge role
parity) are deferred to Brett's design decisions per DECISIONS.md.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Study A review blockers 4-7 to match frozen plan spec

Bring analyze_independent_reassessment.py and build_independent_reassessment.py
implementation in line with what the pre-freeze review already verified as
spec: fixed-denominator reference yield with escape-value exclusion (B4),
majority-baseline/minority-class recall reporting (B5), item-macro accuracy
alongside row-micro (B6), and salt-seeded block permutation plus role-specific
support text (B7). All 7 blockers are now both verified and implemented; the
freeze tag remains Brett's explicit gate.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Verify Study A blockers 4-7 against code before freeze

Blockers 4-6 are one plan-vs-code gap (analyzer doesn't yet implement
the frozen estimand/handling spec); blocker 7 is a real blinding/ordering
gap in the package builder.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add Study A freeze manifest and revision ledger (blocker 8)

scripts/build_study_a_manifest.py hashes the 8 frozen analysis-path
artifacts (plan, schema, both judge comparators, four analysis
scripts) plus the two Ollama model digests into
benchmark/study-a/FREEZE-MANIFEST.json; --verify detects drift.
revision-ledger.md will log any post-freeze deviations. This is
freeze stamp 1 (analysis path); the git tag itself is left as
Brett's explicit pre-registration gate.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Build Study A judge comparators; freeze D4 tasks 1-3

Add scripts/run_study_a_judge.py: grades the 54 pilot outputs under the
Option-A information set (prompt + model output + full v7 codebook, verified
leak-free), reading the label space live from schema.json v7 so escapes are
supported and out-of-space values are rejected rather than silently wrong.
Run two same-family Mistral judges (7B, 24B) deterministically and freeze
their outputs under benchmark/study-a/judge-comparators/.

Extend analyze_independent_reassessment.py with a repeatable --judge-labels
arg so both judges are reported side by side with a judge column instead of
merged.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix judge-column producer/analyzer mismatch; freeze Study A analysis-plan decisions

- analyze_independent_reassessment.py: read judge_<criterion> with bare
  fallback so real LLM-judge producer output scores correctly instead of
  0 eligible rows; raise on duplicate judge rows instead of silent overwrite;
  distinguish available/no_predictions/not_available.
- benchmark/study-a/analysis-plan.md: confirmatory/secondary/exploratory
  estimand table, multiverse spec, small-N inference discipline, and
  hashed-manifest freeze object, incorporating review-board resolution of
  the D4 judge information-set decision (Option A, realistic-deployment).
- DECISIONS.md: log pre-freeze review, blocker verification, and D1-D5
  decisions for Study A.

Bundle arXiv fonts at package top level, not fonts/ subdir

Path=fonts/ with a bundled fonts/ subdir failed to compile on arXiv
("font cannot be found"); bare top-level filenames resolve from the
build CWD and fall back to TeX Live if arXiv strips the binaries.
Verified end-to-end from a clean extraction on both toplevels.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Sync Study A schema v7; add optimizer's-curse evidence discipline

Study A: schema v7 from the full form review (identification-first order,
global judgment last, uniform escape pair, substantive first-slot
none-findings, harmonized labels). Simulator, analyzer, builder, schema.md,
and Study A READMEs synced; annotation-protocol and rater-roles updated;
self-pilot label-diagnostics helper added. v7 form-review note included.

Evidence discipline: add an optimizer's-curse selection-defeater paragraph to
the evidentiary sufficiency test, and a utility/base-rate caveat to the
metrics section. New source-hooks and references-local entries for
Smith & Winkler 2006, Rogard/Gelman/Lu 2007, and the Gelman 2026 post.

Notes: Cognition 2026 intake notes (analytic thinking styles, prospective
relaxation, self-voice illusory truth).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add Study A independent-evaluator workflow and four-field trace schema

Builds the Study A blind-evaluator materials, protocol, and fixtures
(consent, recruitment, self-pilot runbook, schema v6 form generation,
private-boundary and pilot-integrity checks), adds the compositional
delegation section and comparative-test figures, and distributes the
four-field authority/status/fit/action trace schema across the
Delegation Assurance, Evidentiary Assurance, and flagship papers.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Revise assurance paper set

Co-Authored-By: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Add a defeasible action-level sufficiency test and conclusion to the evidentiary assurance draft, and align the abstract and terminology around the new paper structure.
